2008-10-01.title: Somali Pirates Hold Ship
2008-10-01.abstract: Somali pirates continued their seizure of a Ukrainian cargo ship as American warships cornered them.

2008-10-01.title: Mistrust and Hostility Between Two Koreas Shapes Life on an Island
2008-10-01.abstract: Christopher Hill’s trip to North Korea is widely seen as a last-ditch effort by the Bush administration to salvage an agreement on nuclear disarmament.

2008-10-01.title: In Georgia, a Reverence for Stalin
2008-10-01.abstract: An enduring cult of Stalin persists in Georgia, where the Georgian-cobbler’s-son-turned-20th-century-titan remains a towering if contentious figure.

2008-10-01.title: Insurgents in Afghanistan Are Gaining, Petraeus Says
2008-10-01.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus said in an interview that he expected the fight against the insurgents in Afghanistan and Pakistan to get worse before it got better.

2008-10-01.title: In Iran, 3 Degrees of Separation From Truth
2008-10-01.abstract: Iran’s interior minister, Ali Kordan, admitted that a doctorate he said he had earned from Oxford was a fake.

2008-10-02.title: Pakistani Leader Repeats a Long Debunked Hoax
2008-10-02.abstract: In a recent interview, President Asif Ali Zardari seems to believe a hoax alleging that Oliver L. North warned of the dangers posed by Osama bin Laden 20 years ago.

2008-10-02.title: In a Fairy-Tale Village, Russian Orphans Thrive
2008-10-02.abstract: The founders of Kitezh, an experimental orphan community, hope that their village can be a model of reform for Russia’s decrepit child welfare system.

2008-10-02.title: Envoy Pushes to Save Deal on Disarming North Korea
2008-10-02.abstract: Christopher R. Hill extended his trip to Pyongyang in order to salvage a nuclear disarmament deal that the White House had hoped would be a major achievement.

2008-10-02.title: Senate Approves Indian Nuclear Deal
2008-10-02.abstract: Congress gave final approval to a breakthrough agreement permitting civilian nuclear trade between India and the U.S. for the first time in three decades.

2008-10-02.title: More Candy From China, Tainted, Is in U.S.
2008-10-02.abstract: Connecticut’s consumer protection officials issued a public warning against eating contaminated Chinese candy.

2008-10-03.title: Police Chief, Resigning, Cites Mayor of London
2008-10-03.abstract: Sir Ian Blair, known for being a deft politician, has had a rocky time and faced calls for his resignation almost since he took office in February 2005.

2008-10-03.title: Albanians Adjust to Italy, in Unlikeliest of Places
2008-10-03.abstract: Like new shoots grafted onto an old vine, Albanian immigrants are fast becoming an essential part of Italy’s most valued traditions, including winemaking.

2008-10-03.title: As Bombs Fall Silent, an Iraqi City Rebuilds
2008-10-03.abstract: The violence that once raged through Samarra has quieted, yet the costs of greater safety are also apparent.

2008-10-03.title: Mexican President Proposes Decriminalizing Some Drugs
2008-10-03.abstract: Under the proposal, Mexican authorities would not prosecute people found to be carrying small amounts of drugs if they submitted to a treatment program.

2008-10-03.title: Confronting Taliban, Pakistan Finds Itself at War
2008-10-03.abstract: The Pakistani Army is engaged in full-scale battles with militants, and an estimated 250,000 people have fled.

2008-10-04.title: An Egyptian Champion Returns Home to Familiar Challenges
2008-10-04.abstract: In a class-based society where there’s a collective belief that fighting back is futile, Heba Said Ahmed never refers to fate; she talks about choices.

2008-10-04.title: Governor of Afghan Opium Capital Pushes for Crop Replacement
2008-10-04.abstract: The governor of Helmand Province says that he is determined to beat the illicit crop that is a major source of money for drug lords and insurgents alike.

2008-10-04.title: Drug Runners Had Maps of Patrols, Prosecutors Say
2008-10-04.abstract: In 2006, Colombian authorities found a map with the locations of Colombian and United States Navy and Coast Guard patrol boats on a ship smuggling narcotics.

2008-10-04.title: Germans Approach Bailouts With Reluctance
2008-10-04.abstract: Most German citizens are unhappy about the notion of paying billions for bad mortgage bets made by their financial institutions.

2008-10-04.title: Defying a Spate of Bombings, Baghdad Has a Party
2008-10-04.abstract: Baghdad residents seemed determined not to be left out of the festivities of Id al-Fitr, an important holiday that marks the end of Ramadan.

2008-10-05.title: European Leaders Vow to Fight Financial Crisis
2008-10-05.abstract: Despite their pledge, the leaders of Europe’s largest economies failed to offer a systemwide answer to a credit crisis that has forced them to bail out several banks in just the past week.

2008-10-05.title: Russian Forces Begin to Shut Georgia Checkpoints
2008-10-05.abstract: Russian peacekeeping forces dismantled one checkpoint inside Georgia and were at work removing several others on Sunday, five days ahead of a deadline.

2008-10-05.title: Right Thing to Wear at the Wrong End of a Gun
2008-10-05.abstract: As Mexico grapples with an increase in drug-related violence, sales are steadily on the rise at an exclusive boutique, where all the garments are bulletproof.

2008-10-05.title: Women Gaining Rights in Bamian
2008-10-05.abstract: In the Afghan province of Bamian, women are uprooting traditional gender roles by taking up leadership positions.

2008-10-06.title: Kazakhstan Seeks to Balance East and West
2008-10-06.abstract: The resource-rich Central Asian country has hosted leaders from Russia and the United States in recent weeks.

2008-10-06.title: In Poverty and Strife, Women Test Limits
2008-10-06.abstract: In the Afghan province of Bamian, women are uprooting traditional gender roles by taking up leadership positions.

2008-10-06.title: Man Detonates Suicide Vest During U.S. Raid on Iraqi House
2008-10-06.abstract: The Americans said the insurgent they were pursuing in the raid detonated his vest and his companions opened fire. Eleven people were killed in the incident.

2008-10-06.title: Police Detain Protest Leader in Thailand
2008-10-06.abstract: Police arrested Chamlong Srimuang, the leader of the antigovernment protesters occupying the grounds of the prime minister’s office.

2008-10-06.title: Post-Apartheid South Africa Enters Anxious Era
2008-10-06.abstract: South Africa is struggling with high crime, xenophobic violence, and an economic downturn.

2008-10-07.title: Rain Forest Tribe’s Charge of Neglect Is Shrouded by Religion and Politics
2008-10-07.abstract: There is growing concern in Venezuela over indigenous health care after a scandal erupted in August over a tepid official response to a mystery disease.

2008-10-07.title: Islamic Group Gains Power in Indonesia
2008-10-07.abstract: The Council of Ulemas, whose leaders have increasingly espoused a radical form of Islam — has worked to establish itself as an assertive political force.

2008-10-07.title: Iraqis Unite to Restore Minority Representation Law
2008-10-07.abstract: Christians joined by Muslims gathered to demand that the Iraqi Parliament reinstate a version of the provincial elections law that ensured representation for minorities.

2008-10-07.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-10-07.abstract: An article on Friday about the suspected suicide of Choi Jin-sil, a South Korean actress, which the police linked to malicious rumors about her on the Internet, misspelled the name of a South Korean singer who killed herself last year after rumors were spread online that she had undergone plastic surgery. She is Yuni, not Yoo Na.

2008-10-07.title: Rabbi Speaks at the Vatican
2008-10-07.abstract: The first rabbi invited to address the Vatican’s annual synod of bishops criticized President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran.

2008-10-08.title: Putin’s Tips for What to Do When Negotiations Collapse
2008-10-08.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin of Russia is flexing his muscles again, this time in an instructional martial arts video.

2008-10-08.title: U.N. Says Biofuel Subsidies Raise Food Bill and Hunger
2008-10-08.abstract: In a new report, the United Nations joined a number of environmental groups in calling for an end to — or at least an overhaul of — subsidies for biofuels.

2008-10-08.title: An Elite Pastime That Became a Passion of the Masses
2008-10-08.abstract: Brazil’s first truly national soccer museum is a reminder in this soccer-mad nation of how Brazilians became the most successful footballers the world has known.

2008-10-08.title: Gates, in Kosovo, Highlights U.S. Balancing Act on Russia
2008-10-08.abstract: Robert M. Gates’s visit was the first by a U.S. cabinet member since Kosovo’s independence declaration and the first of an American defense secretary since 2001.

2008-10-08.title: 30 Civilians Died in Afghan Raid, U.S. Inquiry Finds
2008-10-08.abstract: A military investigation concluded that recent American airstrikes in western Afghanistan killed more civilians than previously acknowledged.

2008-10-09.title: Pakistani Parliament Briefed by Military
2008-10-09.abstract: In an extraordinary step in Pakistan, the military gave a classified briefing to a special session of Parliament about its efforts in combating militancy and terrorism.

2008-10-09.title: 2 American Journalists Are Said to Be Missing on Lebanon Trip
2008-10-09.abstract: The journalists have not been heard from since Oct. 1 and appear to be missing, according to U.S. officials who appealed for help in locating them.

2008-10-09.title: Russians Vacate Buffer Zones in Georgia
2008-10-09.abstract: Russia removed its last checkpoints from the buffer zones outside the breakaway enclaves of South Ossetia and Abkhazia, fulfilling a key requirement of a cease-fire agreement.

2008-10-09.title: On Capital’s Edge, Gardens That Once Fed an Empire
2008-10-09.abstract: Though the Spanish set about draining the vast lake basin at the center of the vanquished Aztec empire, the canals and their raised farming beds have managed to survive. But now they are under siege.

2008-10-09.title: Serbia Wins Bid to Review Independence of Kosovo
2008-10-09.abstract: In a polarized vote in the U.N. General Assembly, Serbia won a bid to have the World Court review the manner in which Kosovo declared its independence.

2008-10-10.title: Scarred by Past Woes, Japan Sees U.S. Bailout as a First Step
2008-10-10.abstract: Japan’s bitter memories of its banking meltdown in the 1990s made it sympathetic to Washington’s bailout efforts and reluctant to lecture America.

2008-10-10.title: As Fears Ease, Baghdad Sees Walls Tumble
2008-10-10.abstract: The dismantling of blast walls is the most visible sign of change in Baghdad as the surge strategy draws to a close.

2008-10-10.title: A Slow, Wary Return for Georgian Refugees
2008-10-10.abstract: Though government officials urged refugees to wait until their villages have been cleared of unexploded ordnance, it was impossible to stop them from heading home.

2008-10-10.title: Roadside Bomb in Baghdad Kills Shiite Legislator
2008-10-10.abstract: The attack on an Iraqi member of Parliament from Moktada al-Sadr’s political movement prompted supporters of Mr. Sadr to blame the U.S.

2008-10-10.title: In Egypt, End of Hostage Crisis Is a Mystery
2008-10-10.abstract: The circumstances of the release of a group of hostages remain as much a mystery as the identities of the bandits who held them captive last month.

2008-10-11.title: Iran Vendors Protest Move to Collect a Sales Tax
2008-10-11.abstract: The protests, the largest since President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was elected in 2005, occurred in several large cities.

2008-10-11.title: Violence in Mosul Forces Iraqi Christians to Flee
2008-10-11.abstract: At least 11 and perhaps as many as 14 Christians have been killed in the northern Iraqi city since the end of August, according to government officials.

2008-10-11.title: France: Agency Rules That Burqa Violates Values
2008-10-11.abstract: The French agency devoted to combating discrimination has determined that the burqa inhibits integration into French society.

2008-10-11.title: Kyrgyzstan: Leaders Meet, Minus a Few
2008-10-11.abstract: Heads of state from nations in the Commonwealth of Independent States held their first meeting since Georgia quit the organization over its war with Russia.

2008-10-11.title: Mugabe Claims Security Ministries, Jeopardizing Deal
2008-10-11.abstract: Robert Mugabe said that his party will retain ministries that control the military and the police in Zimbabwe.

2008-10-12.title: Massa’s seven-point advantage
2008-10-12.abstract: Yes, Lewis Hamilton is leading the drivers’ series by five points only on Felipe Massa with only two races left. But for the second time in four races, Massa has gained a points advantage thanks to questionable penalties applied to others by the race stewards. And each time, by the way, the penalty was applied in the same manner.

2008-10-12.title: Dark Humor
2008-10-12.abstract: Qassem H.J. is a newspaper cartoonist working in Iraq. He abbreviates his name to protect his identity.

2008-10-12.title: U.S. Declares North Korea Off Terror List
2008-10-12.abstract: North Korea on Sunday welcomed the move and said that it would resume disabling its nuclear weapons facilities.

2008-10-12.title: North Korea to Resume Disabling Nuclear Plant
2008-10-12.abstract: The confirmation comes a day after the United States said it will remove North Korea from its terrorism blacklist.

2008-10-12.title: Zimbabwe’s Power-Sharing Deal Is in Jeopardy
2008-10-12.abstract: Opposition leader Morgan Tsvangirai said his party would walk away from the deal rather than settle for the imbalanced division of ministries handed down by President Robert Mugabe.

2008-10-13.title: Italy’s Attacks on Migrants Fuel Debate on Racism
2008-10-13.abstract: A recent spate of violence against immigrants across Italy is fueling a national conversation about racism and tolerance.

2008-10-13.title: Tax Delay Fails to Quell Iranian Protest
2008-10-13.abstract: A strike in Iran’s traditional bazaars expanded on Sunday despite an order by President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad to suspend a new tax on sales that ignited the protest more than a week ago.

2008-10-13.title: Pakistan President to Visit China, a Valued Ally
2008-10-13.abstract: President Asif Ali Zardari’s visit is an effort to reinforce Pakistan’s ties with a traditional ally at a time when relations with the United States have come under considerable strain.

2008-10-13.title: General Says He’s Hopeful About Taliban War
2008-10-13.abstract: Gen. David D. McKiernan, the top American commander in Afghanistan, urged doubters to believe that the war against the Taliban would be won.

2008-10-13.title: China Announces Land Policy Aimed at Promoting Income Growth in Countryside
2008-10-13.abstract: Chinese leaders said they would adopt a rural growth policy aimed at vastly increasing the income of China’s hundreds of millions of farmers by the year 2020.

2008-10-14.title: Public Stage for Iran’s Ex-President Fuels Talk of Political Return
2008-10-14.abstract: Mohammad Khatami, a political moderate, has hinted that he may run for president in elections next year.

2008-10-14.title: Left-Leaning President’s Election Gives Hope to Landless Paraguayans
2008-10-14.abstract: Landless peasants see President Fernando Lugo as their best chance in decades to help them win back land for small-scale cultivation.

2008-10-14.title: Followers of Ancient Faith Caught in Iraq’s Fault Lines
2008-10-14.abstract: Yazidis, adherents of an ancient religion with roots in Zoroastrianism, were targets of bombings from Sunni extremists, who consider them devil worshipers.

2008-10-14.title: Zimbabwe: Europe Criticizes New Government
2008-10-14.abstract: The E.U. condemned President Robert Mugabe of Zimbabwe for going ahead with the formation of a new government without the agreement of the opposition.

2008-10-14.title: South Africa: A.N.C. Suspends Former Minister
2008-10-14.abstract: The African National Congress suspended Mosiuoa Lekota, who resigned as defense minister, for saying that he might join others in forming a new political party.

2008-10-15.title: Novel About India Wins the Man Booker Prize
2008-10-15.abstract: Aravind Adiga, 33, won the 40th Man Booker prize on Tuesday night for his debut novel, “The White Tiger,” a vivid exploration of India’s class struggle.

2008-10-15.title: British Prime Minister’s Stock Rises as His Bank Plan Lifts Stocks Worldwide
2008-10-15.abstract: After devising a bank rescue plan that has now been endorsed by European and American officials, Gordon Brown is seeing a revival to his political career.

2008-10-15.title: In Tajikistan, Debt-Ridden Farmers Say They Are the Pawns
2008-10-15.abstract: Cotton is at the core of Tajikistan’s economy and farmers are forced to grow cotton through a complex system of debts and obligations.

2008-10-15.title: Russian Gas Executives Visit Palin’s Turf
2008-10-15.abstract: A high-level delegation from the Russian energy company Gazprom met in Anchorage with state officials to talk about investing in Alaskan energy projects.

2008-10-15.title: Corrections
2008-10-15.abstract: Because of an editing error, the Memo from Thailand article on Monday, about political tensions at the core of recent protests, misidentified the main opposition party in Parliament. It is the Democrat Party — not the People’s Alliance for Democracy, a coalition of anti-government protesters.

2008-10-16.title: With a Collective Shrug, Azerbaijan Votes for Its Leader
2008-10-16.abstract: The opposition boycotted the election in protest, saying the government had drawn up rules that made it impossible for it to compete. The catch? Few seemed to care.

2008-10-16.title: Petition Seeks Impeachment of Iranian Interior Minister
2008-10-16.abstract: Members of Parliament petitioned to impeach the minister, Ali Kordan, after investigations indicated that he did not hold academic degrees that he had claimed.

2008-10-16.title: Mob Muscles Its Way Into Politics in Bulgaria
2008-10-16.abstract: Far from halting crime and violence, European Union aid to Bulgaria has effectively helped to spread corruption.

2008-10-16.title: Up North, Hothouse of Tension in Lebanon
2008-10-16.abstract: Behind recent bombings lies an indisputable fact: Sectarian tensions have grown worse in the north, prompting more citizens to arm themselves.

2008-10-16.title: Gates Seeks More Military and Civilian Support in Afghanistan
2008-10-16.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates warned that it “remains to be seen” whether the allies could better coordinate their work in the country.

2008-10-17.title: With Economic Crisis, a Newly Flush India Exercises Caution
2008-10-17.abstract: India’s fast-clip economy is now beginning to show signs of a slowdown, in turn tamping the country’s newfound predilection to spend.

2008-10-17.title: Senior U.S. Commanders to Assess Afghanistan Mission
2008-10-17.abstract: The assessment will be combined into a comprehensive White House review of Afghanistan policy that is to be completed next month after the presidential election.

2008-10-17.title: Bulgarian Prime Minister Vows to Reject Tainted Donations
2008-10-17.abstract: Sergei Stanishev pledged to donate any campaign contributions tainted by irregularities to charity.

2008-10-17.title: European Nations Seek to Revise Agreement on Emissions Cuts
2008-10-17.abstract: Fears of a sharp worldwide economic slowdown are threatening a hard-won European plan on climate change.

2008-10-17.title: Courts Compound Pain of China’s Tainted Milk
2008-10-17.abstract: Tainted infant formula is the latest in a long string of food and drug safety problems that have exposed corruption among China’s regulators.

2008-10-18.title: Symbol of Peace Stands at Divide Between Troubled Jerusalem’s East and West
2008-10-18.abstract: A monument to tolerance was erected recently, seeming to encapsulate both the promise and the fragility of peace in a city increasingly on edge.

2008-10-18.title: Political Turmoil Jeopardizes Financial Relief for Ukraine
2008-10-18.abstract: The International Monetary Fund is seeking assurances from Ukraine’s cabinet about next year’s budget, but President Viktor A. Yushchenko dissolved Parliament and, with it, the cabinet.

2008-10-18.title: Accusation Against Writer Reopens Traumas of Czech Past
2008-10-18.abstract: Life appears to be imitating art in the drama surrounding the accusation that Milan Kundera had denounced a Western intelligence agent.

2008-10-18.title: U.S. Strike Is Said to Kill Qaeda Figure in Pakistan
2008-10-18.abstract: The missile attack killed four people, including a senior member of Al Qaeda, Khalid Habib, who was chief of operations in Pakistan’s tribal region.

2008-10-18.title: Mexico Says U.S. Journalist Was Killed by a Protester
2008-10-18.abstract: The death of Bradley Will had drawn widespread attention because of the Mexican government’s failure to solve a string of slayings of journalists.

2008-10-19.title: A convincing Chinese Grand Prix outcome
2008-10-19.abstract: There have been all sorts of bad feelings, bad vibes and bad thoughts about the results of the recent races and perceived bias towards Ferrari by the International Automobile Federation. But look at today’s race. Who could have imagined that Lewis Hamilton would do all that was necessary to win the championship, while Ferrari, Kimi Raikkonen and Felipe Massa would do all that was necessary to prevent Hamilton from winning. And all just at the absolute limit of what was possible – with the final outcome that Hamilton will go to Sao Paulo not on the top of the world, really, but with the exact same points lead that he had last year before Raikkonen took the championship by one point.

2008-10-19.title: Taliban Kill Dozens in Bus Ambush, Officials Say
2008-10-19.abstract: Afghan officials said that as many as 30 people were beheaded in an attack last week in southern Afghanistan.

2008-10-19.title: Vatican-Israel Tensions Rise Over Pius
2008-10-19.abstract: Tensions heightened this weekend between the Vatican and Israel after a Vatican official said that Pope Benedict XVI had halted the beatification of Pius XII.

2008-10-19.title: China Enacts Major Land-Use Reform
2008-10-19.abstract: The government announced a rural reform policy that for the first time would allow farmers to lease or transfer land-use rights, a step that advocates say would boost lagging incomes.

2008-10-19.title: New Doubts Cloud Iraqi Security Pact With U.S.
2008-10-19.abstract: Several of the leading Iraqi political parties appeared to back away from quick approval of the security agreement between Iraq and the United States.

2008-10-20.title: For Women Who Lead, a Forum of Their Own
2008-10-20.abstract: The Women’s Forum for the Economy and Society included 1,200 women and offered three days of lectures, panels and brainstorming on issues of the moment.

2008-10-20.title: Acclaimed Colombian Institution Has 4,800 Books and 10 Legs
2008-10-20.abstract: A whimsical riff on the bookmobile, Luis Soriano’s “Biblioburro” is a small institution: one man and two donkeys.

2008-10-20.title: Russia Is Striving to Modernize Its Military
2008-10-20.abstract: Pentagon officials portray a Russian military buildup as an overdue modernization, not a threat.

2008-10-20.title: Beyond the Current Crisis, Thai Tensions Run Deep
2008-10-20.abstract: As demonstrations continue, the divisions in Bangkok’s society seem to be deepening, and the mood seems more confrontational and angry.

2008-10-20.title: Zimbabwe Rivals to Discuss Dispute
2008-10-20.abstract: President Robert Mugabe and the opposition leader Morgan Tsvangirai are to meet in Swaziland with other southern African leaders seeking to break a deadlock over power sharing.

2008-10-21.title: Post-Cyclone Aid Divides Myanmar Between the Helped and the Helpless
2008-10-21.abstract: A robust influx of foreign money has showered aid on a small part of Myanmar’s population but has left other, equally desperate parts of the country to fend for themselves.

2008-10-21.title: Pakistani Legislators Show Little Appetite for a Fight
2008-10-21.abstract: Talks in Parliament initiated by President Asif Ali Zardari showed that the political elites had little stomach for battling the militants in the country.

2008-10-21.title: 3 Oil-Rich Countries Face a Reckoning
2008-10-21.abstract: Falling oil prices are raising questions about whether Venezuela, Iran and Russia can sustain their ambitions.

2008-10-21.title: Canada’s Liberal Party Leader Says He Will Step Down
2008-10-21.abstract: Stéphane Dion’s announcement was a new low for a party that has dominated Canada’s politics for much of its history.

2008-10-21.title: Israel: Anti-Pope Image on Web Site
2008-10-21.abstract: An image of Pope Benedict XVI with a swastika over his face appeared on a Web site run by supporters of Kadima, but was removed at the request of the party’s leader.

2008-10-22.title: Iraqi Cabinet Wants Security Agreement Altered
2008-10-22.abstract: Adm. Mike Mullen, chairman of the United States Joint Chiefs of Staff, issued a stark warning to the Iraqis to think hard before rejecting the agreement.

2008-10-22.title: Study Finds Dubious Information Helped Lead to Torture of 3 Canadians
2008-10-22.abstract: Three Canadian men who traveled to Syria independently and for personal reasons were arrested and jailed upon arrival.

2008-10-22.title: Report Details Corruption Cases Involving U.N. Purchasing
2008-10-22.abstract: The report includes several allegations of United Nations employees’ steering contracts to close relatives or to vendors involved in kickback schemes.

2008-10-22.title: Italy’s Crisis Has Premier Riding High
2008-10-22.abstract: Italy’s prime minister, Silvio Berlusconi, is getting a boost to his political fortunes from the economic crisis.

2008-10-22.title: The Netherlands: Cluster Bomb Killed Journalist, Official Says
2008-10-22.abstract: A Russian cluster bomb was responsible for the death of the Dutch journalist Stan Storimans, in Gori, Georgia, on Aug. 12.

2008-10-23.title: A Field of Houses in Georgia Doesn’t Fulfill Dreams of Going Home
2008-10-23.abstract: Two months after the war in South Ossetia, Georgian leaders are on their way to building 7,000 winterized houses, for refugees who will not be able to return home.

2008-10-23.title: A Northern Neighbor Growls, and Azerbaijan Reassesses Its Options
2008-10-23.abstract: Azerbaijan, a small, oil-rich country on the Caspian Sea, has balanced the interests of Russia and the United States since it won its independence from the Soviet Union.

2008-10-23.title: Indonesia: Five Terrorism Suspects Arrested in Raids
2008-10-23.abstract: At least five suspects linked to regional Islamic terrorist groups were arrested in raids in and near Jakarta, the police said Wednesday.

2008-10-23.title: Loose Lips Lead to Trouble for British Tories
2008-10-23.abstract: A breach of elite etiquette leads to questions about whether a prominent British Tory courted Oleg Deripaska, a Russian billionaire, for a donation.

2008-10-23.title: South Africa: Party Leader’s Case Can Be Appealed
2008-10-23.abstract: The judge who threw out corruption charges last month against Jacob Zuma, the leader of the governing party, allowed prosecutors on Wednesday to appeal that decision.

2008-10-24.title: North Korea Faces Worst Food Crisis in Decade
2008-10-24.abstract: Millions of people in the country are facing “severe deprivations” not seen since the mid-1990s, according to a newly released U.N. report.

2008-10-24.title: Pilgrimage to Roots of Faith and Strife
2008-10-24.abstract: At Joseph’s Tomb, a site in a Palestinian city that is sacred to religious Jews, the Israeli-Palestinian conflict is boiled down to its very essence.

2008-10-24.title: Austria Confronts a Leader’s Sexuality
2008-10-24.abstract: Grieving over the death of Jörg Haider, the charismatic far-right politician who died in a car crash, the country has been forced to confront the question of his sexuality.

2008-10-24.title: Drills Heighten Brazil-Paraguay Tensions
2008-10-24.abstract: It was a chilling scene for Paraguayans to watch on television armed Brazilian troops occupying the “Friendship Bridge” separating the two countries.

2008-10-24.title: Greece: Aide Resigns Over Land Swap
2008-10-24.abstract: One of the Greek prime minister’s closest aides resigned over a land scandal that has brought the government to the brink of collapse.

2008-10-25.title: Land Scandal Threatens Greek Leader
2008-10-25.abstract: Two government officials have already resigned over the dealings, in which a wealthy monastery traded cheap tracts of property for prime public real estate.

2008-10-25.title: U.S. Penalizes Companies for Selling Arms Technology
2008-10-25.abstract: A handful of foreign companies have been slapped with sanctions for sales of technologies that could help Iran, Syria and North Korea to develop nuclear weapons.

2008-10-25.title: Complicated Task for Critics of Georgian Leader
2008-10-25.abstract: Georgian opposition figures are split over protests to mark the anniversary of a government crackdown.

2008-10-25.title: In Mozambique, Singing a Song of Sanitation and Hoping to Change Habits
2008-10-25.abstract: While Feliciano dos Santos, one of southern Africa’s leading musicians, sings about love and sorrow, he is better known for songs of a more peculiar sort.

2008-10-25.title: Bail Hearing Delayed for 2 in Zimbabwe
2008-10-25.abstract: Two prominent civic leaders arrested more than a week ago for leading a peaceful protest to demand food for the hungry will spend at least another weekend in jail.

2008-10-26.title: Monetary Fund Won’t Fire Director for Sexual Affair
2008-10-26.abstract: The International Monetary Fund said that it would stand by its managing director despite concluding that he had shown poor judgment in a sexual affair with a subordinate.

2008-10-26.title: Leaders of Europe and Asia Call for Joint Economic Action
2008-10-26.abstract: The event underlined China’s growing role as a diplomatic counterweight to the United States, but fell short of offering specific solutions.

2008-10-26.title: Fury of Girl’s Fists Lifts Up North Korean Refugee Family
2008-10-26.abstract: A girl whose family fled North Korea is breathing new life into boxing by winning a world championship at age 17.

2008-10-26.title: Live Blogging an Embed in Ramadi
2008-10-26.abstract: Throughout each day I’ll provide updates on the Baghdad Bureau blog that will explore the everyday lives of American soldiers who remain stationed in a region that is maintaining a fragile calm.

2008-10-26.title: As Israeli Elections Are Called, Livni Is Assessed
2008-10-26.abstract: Tzipi Livni’s critics said her failure to form a government proved a lack of political savvy and experience.

2008-10-27.title: A French Family Dynasty Reinvents the Oyster
2008-10-27.abstract: The fourth generation of the Gillardeau family has modernized and expanded the company, in an oyster industry that has been reluctant to change.

2008-10-27.title: Russians React to Article on Moscow Mayor’s Ventures
2008-10-27.abstract: Russians were sharply divided in response to an article in The New York Times that explored the role of Yuri M. Luzhkov in promoting the Kremlin’s aggressive foreign policy.

2008-10-27.title: Ex-Militant Concedes Loss in Election for Rio Mayor
2008-10-27.abstract: Fernando Gabeira, a Brazilian congressman who helped kidnap an American ambassador during Brazil’s military dictatorship, came up short in his bid to be elected mayor of Rio de Janeiro.

2008-10-27.title: Somalia Makes Peace Deal With a Militia
2008-10-27.abstract: Somalia’s transitional leaders agreed to accept insurgent troops within their ranks and detailed a plan for a phased pullback of Ethiopian soldiers.

2008-10-27.title: Tainted Eggs From China Discovered in Hong Kong
2008-10-27.abstract: Food inspectors have found eggs imported from northeast China to be contaminated with high levels of melamine, the chemical at the heart of an adulteration scandal involving Chinese milk products.

2008-10-28.title: Refuse Heap Is Archive for Night of Hatred
2008-10-28.abstract: A trove of evidence of a night in which rioters wreaked havoc on Jewish neighborhoods has been found in the local trash dump in a town in Germany.

2008-10-28.title: Former Official of U.N. Tribunal Declines to Plead in Contempt Case
2008-10-28.abstract: Florence Hartmann was charged for discussing confidential rulings by appeals judges in a book she wrote after leaving her U.N. post.

2008-10-28.title: Rate of Nuclear Thefts ‘Disturbingly High,’ Monitoring Chief Says
2008-10-28.abstract: The chief of the International Atomic Energy Agency said nearly 250 thefts of nuclear or radioactive material were reported in the year ending in June.

2008-10-28.title: Live Blogging an Embed From Forward Operating Base Tash
2008-10-28.abstract: The embed destination is a muddied joint security station run by a Marine rifle company that is located a few miles outside of Ramadi.

2008-10-28.title: Officials Say Drug Cartels Infiltrated Mexican Law Unit
2008-10-28.abstract: Five officials in the attorney general’s elite organized crime unit, receiving as much as $450,000 a month to feed secrets to a major cartel, were arrested.

2008-10-29.title: In World’s Largest Democracy, Tolerance Is a Weak Pillar
2008-10-29.abstract: India is reeling from a rash of religious and ethnic clashes, prompting many in the country to ask why their democracy tends to encourage intolerance.

2008-10-29.title: Russia Seeks to Trade Oil for Loans From China
2008-10-29.abstract: Under a proposed deal, Russian oil companies would borrow $20 billion to $30 billion from Beijing. In return, they would export about two billion barrels of oil to China.

2008-10-29.title: Iran Opens Naval Base Near Routes for Gulf Oil
2008-10-29.abstract: An Iranian naval commander said that Iran had created the base to counter hostile forces, in what was clearly an allusion to American Navy vessels patrolling nearby.

2008-10-29.title: Slowdown in Persian Gulf Reverberates in Middle East
2008-10-29.abstract: A slowdown in the Persian Gulf might feel like a crash landing in places like Egypt, Jordan and Syria, where gulf money has helped prop up strained economies.

2008-10-29.title: Armored Against Turmoil, Lebanon Lures Investors
2008-10-29.abstract: Lebanon, chronically battered by war, turns out to have a banking sector that has so far been a beacon of stability and growth.

2008-10-30.title: Later Today: Photographer’s Journal Part II
2008-10-30.abstract: Later today, in the second feature of our new series ‘Photographer’s Journal’, Marko Georgiev will write on working during one of Iraq’s extraordinary sandstorms.

2008-10-30.title: Sarkozy Boldly Attacks Financial Crisis, but Europe Wants Results
2008-10-30.abstract: Initially praised for his leadership and quick response to the crisis, the French president, Nicolas Sarkozy, is facing increasing pressure as the European recession gathers pace.

2008-10-30.title: Find of Ancient City Could Alter Notions of Biblical David
2008-10-30.abstract: Archaeologists are unearthing a city overlooking the Valley of Elah that could reshape views of the controversial period when David ruled over the Israelites.

2008-10-30.title: Afghanistan Tests Waters for Overture to Taliban
2008-10-30.abstract: The Afghan government and its allies in the region have begun approaching the Taliban and other insurgent groups to test the possibilities for eventual peace talks.

2008-10-30.title: New Spin on Cuba Embargo Resolution
2008-10-30.abstract: Cuba introduced a slight twist at the United Nations General Assembly on Wednesday to its 17th annual resolution calling for an end to the American economic embargo.

2008-10-31.title: Crowds Bid Farewell to Airport That Saved Berlin
2008-10-31.abstract: Tempelhof, although built by the Nazis, is best known as the site of the Berlin airlift of 1948 and 1949, after the Soviets blocked land access to the city.

2008-10-31.title: Discord in South Africa’s Top Party Sows Seeds of a Rival
2008-10-31.abstract: During the past few weeks, several prominent defectors from the African National Congress have been fomenting political rebellion.

2008-10-31.title: Iraqi Ministry Adopts Political Neutrality
2008-10-31.abstract: The announcement made by Iraq’s defense minister would affect all employees of the ministry, from the highest officials to the lowliest soldiers.

2008-10-31.title: South Koreans Lose Faith in President’s Business Skills
2008-10-31.abstract: Critics say Lee Myung-bak and his cabinet responded slowly to the financial crisis, which has struck hard at South Korea and other emerging market nations.

2008-10-31.title: McCain and Obama Advisers Briefed on Deteriorating Afghan War
2008-10-31.abstract: Experts sought to make clear to advisers from the presidential campaigns that the next president needed to have a plan for Afghanistan before he took office on Jan. 20.
